title: Produce + publish ~10 chapters (first repeatable run)
context: After the M1 pipeline adapters are operational, this task validates that the entire pipeline is repeatable and policy-compliant at a modest scale of ~10 chapters. It exercises the rights gate, consent gate, segmentation, pronunciation lexicon, audio mastering, forced alignment, human QA rotation, and publish adapter end-to-end. Every item must pass all gates and carry the human/synthetic label. The acceptance rate and median alignment error are measured to establish pipeline maturity before scaling further. Depends on adapter-001, adapter-002, adapter-003, pipeline-001, pipeline-002, and doc-001.
objective: Run the M1 pipeline end-to-end to produce and publish approximately 10 chapters across ≥1 work/collection, measuring acceptance rate and alignment accuracy to validate pipeline maturity.
acceptanceCriteria: ≥10 chapters are published upstream at the confirmed channel, live and playable, within 1–2 works/collections.
Every chapter passed in sequence: rights gate → consent gate → pronunciation-lexicon review → audio-quality check → fidelity/pronunciation/audio/alignment QA → publish adapter.
Every published chapter carries the correct human/synthetic label, license, attribution, speaker-consent reference, and provenance.
The acceptance rate (published ÷ submitted) is measured and recorded.
The median sentence/word onset alignment error is measured against the narrate-001 reference set and recorded.
Zero rights, consent, or labeling violations.
output: ≥10 chapters published at the confirmed upstream channel (live URLs / PR / deposit references), updated per-work records for each chapter, a batch-001 run report recording acceptance rate, median alignment error, and any gate rejections.
